description: A personal essay collection and structured knowledge graph mapping the three major Buddhist Abhidharma traditions (Theravāda, Sarvāstivāda, Yogācāra) onto contemporary frameworks in computational phenomenology — predictive processing, active inference, Global Workspace Theory, Parallel Distributed Processing, and related approaches. Comprises fourteen essays (seven themes × English + Japanese), a cross-tradition dharma comparison table (~130 dharmas across nine sheets), and a JSON-LD knowledge graph (~284 nodes, schema.org + custom vocabulary) with bilingual labels and Sanskrit / Pāli / Chinese philological alignment. An interactive 2D/3D viewer is provided. v0.3.0 adds Theme 7 (Cessation and continuity without a self) — meditative cessation (nirodha samāpatti) as the sharpest form of the no-self continuity problem, set against four Abhidharma answers to what carries continuity across the gap (Sarvāstivāda prāpti / Sautrāntika bīja / Yogācāra ālaya-vijñāna / Theravāda bhavaṅga) and Laukkonen et al. 2023's active-inference model of cessation. It also relicenses the repository to CC0 1.0 and federates the line into the shimo4228 research-program hub. Developed in collaboration with the Claude language model (Anthropic) for synthesis, cross-tradition mapping, and graph encoding; the author retains responsibility for all claims.
name: Attention, Not Self: Buddhist Abhidharma Meets Computational Phenomenology
